title: 6-Cube Organizer Bookshelf
summary: A six-compartment 1x12 bookshelf on short legs, framed with 1x2 trim on the front and back for extra rigidity.
description:
This is a straightforward grid bookshelf — a top, two sides, one center shelf, and vertical dividers splitting it into six equal cubbies — with a detail that keeps a piece this size from racking: 1x2 trim strips nailed and glued across the front and back, tying the whole frame together after the main joints are set. Attach that trim after the basic box and dividers are assembled and square, not before, since it's meant to lock the shape in rather than help build it.

Where the vertical dividers meet the shelves, apply glue on top of your screws — that's the joint doing the most work in the whole piece, since it's what keeps the shelf spans from sagging once loaded with books.

tools.note: Alternative to countersunk screws for the main joints.

materials.name: 2" self-tapping wood screws or pocket hole screws

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit — lengths will vary slightly.

steps.title: Mark and drill the joints
steps.body: Mark out all your joint locations on the cut pieces. Predrill and countersink for 2" screws and glue throughout, or use a pocket-hole jig with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws instead.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf and dividers
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf's divider pieces to the bottom shelf with 2" screws. Attach the bottom shelf to the sides, applying glue at every point where a vertical cubby divider will line up — a 1x2 leg will reinforce this joint in a later step, but the glue here is what carries the shelf load long-term.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the sides, flush on all sides, with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the front and back trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the horizontal trim pieces to fit the front of the bookshelf, then repeat for the back. This trim ties the whole box together and is what keeps it from racking.

steps.title: Attach the back panel trim and finish
steps.body: Attach the back trim with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Sand, fill nail holes, and finish.

images.alt: A six-cube storage bookshelf built from 1x12 boards
